Another statement in the record impinges on this matter. When Mosiah 25:2 speaks of the subjects ruled by Mosiah, it contrasts two categories of the population. The first is, of course, "the children of Nephi . . . who were descendants of Nephi," that is, apparently, those who had arrived in the land of Zarahemla guided by the first King Mosiah. The Immediate Goodness of God By Elder Kyle S. McKay Of the Seventy Even while we are patiently waiting upon the Lord, there are certain blessings that come to us immediately. Several years ago, our five-year-old son came to me and announced, “Dad, I’ve figured something out. was called the land of Mormon; yea, and he did baptize his brethren in the waters of Mormon.
was called the land of Mormon; yea, and he did baptize his brethren in the waters of Mormon.

As many as he did baptize did belong to the church] Those worthily baptized obtain, through this ordinance, membership in the Church and thus citizenship in both the earthly and heavenly kingdoms of God. (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L. Millet, Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon, 4 vols., 2:, p.291)
